name: Astorga from Bembibre – circular via Molinaseca and Palacio de Gaudí – Eurovelo 3
name_original: Astorga desde Bembibre – circular por Molinaseca y Palacio de Gaudí – Eurovelo 3
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Bembibre traverse the fertile Bercian valley, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region is characterized by a mix of open valleys and lush forests, with the Bouza River influencing some routes. This varied terrain provides options for different cycling preferences, from routes winding through wooded areas to those crossing scenic agricultural lands. Best road cycling routes around Bembibre The most popular road cycling route is Astorga from Bembibre – circular via Molinaseca and Palacio de Gaudí – Eurovelo 3, a 70.0 miles (112.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 36 minutes to complete. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain and passes by historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Folgoso de la Ribera – View of Bembibre loop from Bembibre, a moderate 39.1 miles (63.0 km) path. It offers views of the surrounding valley and varied road cycling terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Toreno – Ponferrada Castle loop from Bembibre, a 40.4 miles (65.1 km) trail leading through the fertile Bercian valley, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Bembibre is defined by fertile valleys, lush forests, and riverine landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult routes with significant elevation profiles. The routes in Bembibre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bembibre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bembibre?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Bembibre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from moderate loops to more challenging rides with significant elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Bembibre?
A: Road cycling in Bembibre features diverse terrain. Routes traverse the fertile Bercian valley, offering a mix of open agricultural lands and lush forests. You can expect varied landscapes, including sections along the Bouza River, providing both flat stretches and climbs with notable elevation profiles.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Bembibre?
A: While many routes in Bembibre are moderate to difficult, there are a few easier options suitable for families or those new to road cycling. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, allowing for a more relaxed experience through the scenic valley.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by interesting sights. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046042403/astorga-from-bembibre-circular-via-molinaseca-and-palacio-de-gaudi-eurovelo-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astorga from Bembibre – circular via Molinaseca and Palacio de Gaudí – Eurovelo 3&lt;/a&gt; takes you past historical landmarks. Other routes might offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6183733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Dam on the Sil River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has a Romanesque church and the Sanctuary of Ecce Homo, which can be incorporated into rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Bembibre?
A: Yes, Bembibre is a great starting point for circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046042403/astorga-from-bembibre-circular-via-molinaseca-and-palacio-de-gaudi-eurovelo-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astorga from Bembibre – circular via Molinaseca and Palacio de Gaudí – Eurovelo 3&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 70-mile loop. Another local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33676246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgoso de la Ribera – View of Bembibre loop from Bembibre&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 39-mile path.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bembibre?
A: The fertile Bercian valley generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be good, but it's advisable to start earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Bembibre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from open valleys to lush forests, and the variety of routes that cater to different ability levels, including challenging climbs and scenic loops.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass by waterfalls or natural monuments?
A: Yes, the Bembibre area is home to several natural attractions. While not directly on every road cycling route, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Hoyas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sulphur Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zofreral de Arriba forest&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest in the wider region.

Q: Are there any specific routes for advanced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. For experienced road cyclists, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046042403/astorga-from-bembibre-circular-via-molinaseca-and-palacio-de-gaudi-eurovelo-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astorga from Bembibre – circular via Molinaseca and Palacio de Gaudí – Eurovelo 3&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and longer distances, providing a demanding and rewarding experience. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponferrada Castle – Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross) loop from Bembibre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available in Bembibre for cyclists, and can I bring my bike?
A: Bembibre has public transport options, but policies regarding bikes can vary. It's recommended to check with local bus or train operators directly for their specific rules on carrying bicycles, especially during peak times or for longer journeys.

Q: Are there any specific parking facilities for cyclists in Bembibre?
A: While there isn't dedicated 'cyclist-only' parking, Bembibre offers various municipal parking areas where you can safely leave your vehicle before starting a ride. Look for general public parking facilities within the town center or near the main access points to routes.

Q: Are cycling permits required for road cycling in the Bembibre area or Castilla y León?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads in the Bembibre area or the wider Castilla y León region of Spain. Cyclists are expected to follow standard traffic laws and regulations.
tips: The small Bercian town of Molinaseca, located a few kilometers from Ponferrada, is one of the most important enclaves on the Jacobean Route. Its streets with a medieval flavor invite you to stroll slowly, not in vain, in 1975 it was declared a Historic-Artistic Site.

The Cruz de Ferro marks the highest point of the Camino Frances de Santiago. At the top of the port of Foncebadón, at 1504 meters.
Around the wooden post on which the cross is located, you will see a mountain of stones. Tradition makes pilgrims deposit here a stone brought from their place of origin.
Santa Colomba de Somoza is a small town on the banks of the Turienzo River and an important trade route between Ponferrada and Astorga.
Murias de Rechivaldo is located on the banks of the Jerga River and preserves the only mill on the river in excellent condition.

Gaudí's episcopal palace, in Astorga, is a neo-Gothic style building designed by the modernist architect Antonio Gaudí. Due to its uniqueness, it constitutes one of the most outstanding buildings in Astorga's monumental heritage. In 1963 it became the Caminos Museum, and numerous and outstanding pieces from the Diocese of Astorga are exhibited, including prehistory, epigraphy and Roman numismatics, religious art from various periods and contemporary art.
Bembibre is an administrative center and regional city of the Bierzo region. The city is situated on an important trade route along the Beoza river between Ponferrada and Astorga
